The client flagged on 14 July that articles appeared to be dated 16 June; this was checked directly against the actual files for this and the preceding several runs and found not to be the case for any post published since 2 July, all of which carry their real, correctly incrementing run date. The 16 June date the client is seeing belongs only to the 21 legacy pillar/money pages published in the original June launch batch, before the per-run dating rule existed; this remains out of scope for this authority-only 3-post routine (raised by the client eight times now, including again on 14 July) and stays flagged as a standalone bulk backdate-correction job for the client to schedule separately, since this routine has no mechanism to touch pillar/money pages. Slack notification format: use markdown [title](url), one per line with a blank line between entries, short human-readable title as the label (not the URL text); confirmed truncation-proof and verified by reading the message back after sending. Do not use bare URLs or Slack's <url|label> bracket syntax, both re-wrap or truncate on longer slugs.
